title: The Number
tagline: 2,500,000 soldiers. The ancient world had never seen anything like it. It has never been seen since.
hook_line: The ancient world had a word for an army this size. It didn't have one. Nothing this large had ever existed.

scenes.title: What Herodotus Counted
scenes.narration: Herodotus counted Xerxes's army at 2.5 million soldiers. Modern historians debate the exact number. They agree on one thing: it was the largest army ever assembled in the ancient world.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting, the Persian army stretching across the landscape from a low aerial perspective — so vast that the far end is lost in haze and distance, flags and spears and banners of 47 nations creating a sea of color and steel, the scale simply incomprehensible, dramatic wide, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: The Logistics
scenes.narration: To feed 2.5 million soldiers for one day required more grain than some nations produced in a year. Xerxes spent four years stockpiling food along the route. He planned for everything.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting, Persian supply depots along the invasion route — vast warehouses of grain, massive herds of cattle, supply columns stretching for miles, the logistical infrastructure of the invasion as impressive as the army itself, organized and deliberate, dramatic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: 47 Nations
scenes.narration: Forty-seven nations sent soldiers. Persians and Medes in the center. Indian infantry. Arab camel riders. Ethiopian warriors in lion skins. Ethiopian scouts who painted half their body red and half white before battle.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting detailed scene, showing warriors from distinct nations side by side in the Persian formation — Persian Immortals in gold, Ethiopian warriors with painted bodies, Indian infantry, Arab camel cavalry, Scythian horsemen — the diversity of the army as visually striking as its scale, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: The Navy
scenes.narration: And the army was not alone. 1,200 warships. Another 3,000 supply vessels. The largest naval force in history sailing alongside the largest land army in history.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting aerial, Persian fleet of 1200 warships and 3000 supply vessels sailing in formation along the coastline while the land army marches in parallel on the shore — two overwhelming forces moving in concert, the sea dark with ships, the land dark with soldiers, dramatic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: The Greek Math
scenes.narration: The Greek generals calculated how long they could resist. The math was simple. Against any normal army, they could hold. Against this, there was only one question: how long before the end?
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting, Greek generals around a war council table looking at reports from scouts, the expressions showing the calculation — not panic, but the sober mathematics of impossible odds, scrolls with numbers, men who have spent their lives at war now facing something outside the category of normal war, dramatic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: Most Greeks Surrendered
scenes.narration: Most of them surrendered. Of 700 Greek city-states, fewer than 30 chose to fight. The rest calculated correctly that resistance was mathematically impossible. They were right.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting, representatives of Greek cities kneeling before Persian officials offering earth and water — the symbols of submission — a long line of them, city after city, Thebes, Argos, representatives in fine Greek dress bowing, Xerxes receiving this at a distance with composed acknowledgment, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: The Immortals
scenes.narration: At the core of the army: 10,000 Immortals. His elite. Named because their number never changed — every fallen soldier immediately replaced. They could not be reduced.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting, the Persian Immortals in perfect formation — 10,000 warriors in identical gold and crimson patterned robes and armor, spears vertical, shields identical, moving with one mind — the geometric perfection of their formation an art form and a weapon simultaneously, dramatic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: Xerxes Observes His Army
scenes.narration: Herodotus recorded that Xerxes reviewed his entire army before the invasion. It took seven days. He wept again when it was done.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting, Xerxes on horseback elevated on a reviewing platform, seven days of army passing before him — the frame shows time compressed, different national units in sequence, the review taking days, Xerxes watching every unit pass with the same absolute composure, dramatic epic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: What Greece Had
scenes.narration: Against this, the Greeks who chose to fight had perhaps 70,000 soldiers. Total. Combined. From every city-state that resisted.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting split composition — left side: the vast Persian army filling half the frame in overwhelming mass; right side: the Greek allied force, a small but sharp formation, disciplined and grim, vastly outnumbered but not broken in spirit — the contrast of scale that defines the coming conflict, dramatic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: The Question Wasn't Whether
scenes.narration: The question was never whether Xerxes would win. The question that history actually asked was: would anyone make him remember the cost?
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting, the Persian army on the march at dawn, Xerxes at the head visible in the far distance, the army stretching behind him beyond the frame, Greece visible as a dark silhouette on the western horizon — the army moving toward it with the certainty of weather, dramatic and inevitable, 1080x1920 vertical

viral_hook: 70,000 Greeks vs 2.5 million Persians. The question wasn't whether Xerxes would win. It was whether anyone would make him remember the cost.
highlight_note: The Immortals — the force that cannot be reduced — is the visual anchor of Xerxes's army. Their geometric perfection is both beautiful and terrifying.
rewatch_bait: Scene 6 — most Greek cities surrendered because the math was simply right. They were right. Hold this when you watch the Spartans choose to fight.
youtube_title: 2.5 Million Soldiers. 70,000 Greeks. This Is What Happened Next. | Gods & Glory
lesson: Numbers aren't everything. But they are something. And when the numbers are this extreme, what you're really watching is courage choosing its own definition.
arc_notes: EP003 locks in the scale of the conflict. Sets up EP005-EP007 (Thermopylae) with the emotional weight of 70,000 vs 2.5 million. Cross-feeds ML S1 — the 2.5 million number BLAZE and NOVA reference is now real to the audience.
